title: SailCompass: Towards Reproducible and Robust Evaluation for Southeast Asian Languages

task_A_numeric.topic: What does this paper claim about the effect of manipulated training on model performance with the LiTiLo prompt configuration?
task_A_numeric.exact_quote: With manipulated training, the model shows a significant 17.2% improvement with prompt LiTiLo.

task_A_prose.topic: What does this paper claim about the importance of balanced language distribution for SEA LLM development?
task_A_prose.exact_quote: A balanced language distribution is important for developing better SEA-specialized LLMs.

task_B.topic: which prompt language works better for SEA language models
task_B.true_claim: English prompts generally outperform native prompts, but Sea-Lion is an exception because its pretraining corpus is SEA-heavy, and the gap is small enough (<0.5 Chrf++) to be acceptable either way.

task_C.figure: Table 3 (MCQ benchmark results on BELEBELE with prompt variants)
task_C.exact_command: python evaluate.py --model seallm --lang id vi th --tasks all
task_C.note: repo exists with eval scripts, command not in paper prose
